---
title: "GP Drying Events"
author: "CLB"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(here)

source(here("Code/paths+packages.R"))
#source(here("Code/priceDrying.R"))
#source(here("Code/Functions/exoPipeline.R"))
#source(here("Code/Functions/BaseflowSeparation.R"))
library(preMetabolizer)
```

```{r}

siteInfo <- read_csv(here("Metadata/AIMS_Site_Meta.csv")) |> 
  filter(siteId %in% c("SFM01", "SHM01", "ENM01", "TLM01", "WHM01", "PRM01", "JSS01", "DSS01", "GSS01"))
```

# Q Data

```{r}
########### Discharge from PT Pipeline ########### 
gpQ <- aims_read(input_dir = "~/Dropbox/AIMS/GP_SW-GW_QAQC/Data/Processed", rType = "DISC") |> 
  rename(dateTime = datetime, Site = siteId) |> 
  mutate(Site = case_when(Site == "ENM01" ~ "YMR",
                          Site == "SFM01" ~ "KMZ",
                          Site == "SHM01" ~ "SHN")) |> 
  select(Site, dateTime, Q_Ls)

gpPT <- aims_read(input_dir = "~/Dropbox/AIMS/GP_SW-GW_QAQC/Data/Processed", rType = "PRES") |> 
  rename(dateTime = datetime, Site = siteId) |> 
  mutate(Site = case_when(Site == "ENM01" ~ "YMR",
                          Site == "SFM01" ~ "KMZ",
                          Site == "SHM01" ~ "SHN")) |> 
  select(Site, dateTime, sublocation, waterDepth_m, wetdry) |> 
   pivot_wider(names_from = sublocation, values_from = c(waterDepth_m, wetdry))

gpQ <- gpQ |> 
  full_join(gpPT, by = c("Site", "dateTime")) |> 
  group_by(Site) |>
  complete(dateTime = seq(floor_date(min(dateTime), "10 min"), floor_date(max(dateTime), "10 min"), by = "10 min")) |>
  arrange(dateTime) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(wetdry_SW = ifelse(wetdry_SW == "", NA, wetdry_SW)) |> 
  replace_na(list(Q_Ls = 0, waterDepth_m_SW = 0)) |> 
  mutate(wetQf = case_when(
    (Q_Ls <= 0 & wetdry_SW == "wet") ~ "pooled",
    Q_Ls > 0 & wetdry_SW == "wet" ~ "flowing",
    wetdry_SW == "dry" ~ "dry",
    is.na(wetdry_SW) ~ "no data",
    TRUE ~ NA  # Keep NA values as NA
  )) |> 
  rename(swDepth = waterDepth_m_SW, gwDepth = waterDepth_m_PI)


#approach 2 metadata (use for dry/wet at site interpretation: if Date is present but Temp is NA, site is dry
# Approach 2 Metadata
appr2 <- read_csv(here("Metadata/Great Plains Seasonal Sampling Field Data.csv")) |>
  filter(Site %in% c("SFM01", "ENM01", "SHM01")) |>
  mutate(Date = mdy(Date),
         wetdry = ifelse(is.finite(Date) & is.finite(`Temp (*C)`), "wet",
                         ifelse(is.na(Date), "NA", "dry")),
         wetQf = case_when(grepl("Y", `Connected?`) & grepl("Y", `Flowing?`) ~ "Flowing",
                           grepl("N", `Connected?`) & grepl("N", `Flowing?`) ~ "Pooled",
                           grepl("Y", `Connected?`) & grepl("N", `Flowing?`) ~ "Pooled",
                           grepl("N", `Connected?`) & grepl("Y", `Flowing?`) ~ "Pooled",
                           is.na(`Connected?`) & is.na(`Flowing?`) ~ "Dry")) |>
  mutate(Site = case_when(Site == "ENM01" ~ "YMR",
                          Site == "SFM01" ~ "KMZ",
                          Site == "SHM01" ~ "SHN")) |>
    select(Site, Date, Connected = `Connected?`, Flowing = `Flowing?`, wetdry, wetQf)

## Sensor Maintence 
sensMaint <- read_csv(here("Metadata/SensorMaint.csv")) |> 
  mutate(Date = mdy(`Date of Check`),
         wetdry = ifelse(is.finite(Date) & is.finite(`Temp (*C)`), "wet", 
                         ifelse(is.na(Date), "NA", "dry")),
         wetQf = case_when(grepl("Y", `Connected?`) & grepl("Y", `Flowing?`) ~ "Flowing",
                           grepl("N", `Connected?`) & grepl("N", `Flowing?`) ~ "Pooled",
                           grepl("Y", `Connected?`) & grepl("N", `Flowing?`) ~ "Pooled",
                           grepl("N", `Connected?`) & grepl("Y", `Flowing?`) ~ "Pooled",
                           grepl("D", `Connected?`) & grepl("D", `Flowing?`) ~ "Dry")) |> 
  select(Site, Date, Connected = `Connected?`, Flowing = `Flowing?`, wetdry, wetQf)

flowMeta <- sensMaint |> 
  full_join(appr2) |>
  filter(!is.na(wetQf)) |> 
  select(Site, Date, wetQf)
```

```{r}
t <- gpQ |> 
  filter(is.na(wetQf))
# 
# # Convert dateTime to Date
# flowState <- gpQ |>
#   mutate(Date = as.Date(dateTime)) |>
#   group_by(Site, Date) |>
#   summarise(flowState = names(sort(table(wetQf), decreasing = TRUE)[1]))

# Get most freq flowstate for each site and date
flowState <- gpQ |>
  mutate(Date = as.Date(dateTime)) |> # Extract the date from dateTime
  group_by(Site, Date, wetQf) |>      # Group by Site, date, and wetQf
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") |> # Count occurrences
  group_by(Site, Date) |>            # Group by Site and date
  slice_max(count, n = 1, with_ties = F) |> # Get the most frequent wetQf
  ungroup()  |>                          # Remove grouping
  select(Site, Date, flowState = wetQf, -count)
```

# DO Data

```{r}
# Path to QAQC pipeline
qaqcPath <- here("Data/EXO_QAQC/01_data/04_postQaqc/")
# Site to load data
Sites <- c("SHN", "KMZ", "YMR")
# Read data for each site and combine
gpDO <- aims_read(input_dir = qaqcPath, siteId = Sites, rType = "EXOS") |> 
  filter(alphaQf == 0 & betaQfSd == 0) |>
  select(Site, dateTime = timestamp, parameterName, value) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = parameterName,
              values_from = value,
              values_fn = mean) |>
  mutate(
    dateTime = parse_date_time(dateTime, orders = c("ymd HMS"), tz = "UTC"),
    ODO_mg_L = if_else(ODO_mg_L == 0, NA, ODO_mg_L)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    DO.obs = coalesce(ODO_mg_L, ODO_MiniDOT_mg_L),
    temp.water = coalesce(Temp_EXO_C, `Temp_s-can_C`, SW_Temp_PT_C, Temp_MiniDOT_C)
  ) |>
  select(Site, dateTime, DO.obs, temp.water) |>
  mutate(DO.obs = ifelse(DO.obs > 15, NA, DO.obs)) |> 
  filter(!(Site == "YMR" & dateTime < "2021-07-18"),
         !(Site == "SHN" & dateTime < "2023-03-24"),
         !(Site == "KMZ" & dateTime < "2021-06-10")
         )

gpDO <- gpDO |>
  group_by(Site) |>
  complete(dateTime = seq(floor_date(min(dateTime), "10 min"), floor_date(max(dateTime), "10 min"), by = "10 min")) |>
  arrange(dateTime) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na.approx(.x, na.rm = F, maxgap = 10))) |>
  filter(minute(dateTime) %% 10 == 0) |>
  ungroup()

gpDO |>
  ggplot(aes(dateTime, DO.obs)) +
  geom_line(color = "#0084a8") +
  ylab("DO (mg/L)") +
  theme_few() +
  scale_x_datetime(labels = date_format("%b '%y")) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))+
  facet_wrap(~Site, ncol = 2, scales = "free")
```

# BP Data

```{r}
#Howard 14N elevation
ymrStnElev <- 1581 * 0.3048 #ft to m
ymrElev <- siteInfo |> 
  filter(siteId == "ENM01") |> 
  pull(Elev_m)
# Howard 14NW Met Station
ymrBP <- read_csv("~/Dropbox/AIMS/Data/YMR/ymrMesonet.csv") |> 
  rename(dateTime = DateTime_UTC) |> 
  mutate(
    Pres_kPa = if_else(grepl("Pres", Flag), NA_real_, Pres_kPa),
    Temp_C = if_else(grepl("Temp", Flag), NA_real_, Temp_C),
    RH_pct = if_else(grepl("RH_p", Flag), NA_real_, RH_pct),
    ShortRad_mJ_m2 = if_else(grepl("Shor", Flag), NA_real_, ShortRad_mJ_m2)
  ) |> 
  select(-Flag, -stationName, -Lat, -Long) |> 
  complete(dateTime = seq(floor_date(min(dateTime), "10 min"), floor_date(max(dateTime), "10 min"), by = "10 min")) |> 
  fill( Elev_m) |> 
  arrange(dateTime) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~zoo::na.approx(.x, na.rm = F, maxgap = 12))) |> 
  mutate(bp_kPa.cor = correct_bp(Pres_kPa, Temp_C, Elev_m, ymrElev),
         Site = "YMR") |> 
  select(Site, dateTime, bp_kPa = Pres_kPa, bp_kPa.cor, airTempC = Temp_C)

neonDP <- neon_products() |> 
  filter(productName %in% c("Barometric pressure", "Single aspirated air temperature", "Air temperature above water on-buoy")) |> 
  pull(productCode)

#download data
neon_download(
  product = neonDP,
  start_date = "2021-01-01",
  end_date = "2024-12-31",
  site = c("KONZ", "KING"),
  .token = Sys.getenv("NEON_Token"))

neon_store(product = neonDP)

neonBP <- neon_table(table = "BP_1min-basic", site = c("KONZ"), lazy = F) |> 
  filter(minute(startDateTime) %% 10 == 0 & staPresFinalQF == 0) |> 
  select(startDateTime, siteID, staPresMean) |> 
  collect()

neonTemp <- neon_table(table = "TAAT_1min-basic", site = c("KONZ"), lazy = F) |> 
  filter(minute(startDateTime) %% 10 == 0 & finalQF == 0) |> 
  select(startDateTime, siteID, tempTripleMean) |> 
  collect()


neonDat <- neonBP |> 
  full_join(neonTemp) |> 
  select(dateTime = startDateTime, siteID, bp_kPa = staPresMean, airTempC = tempTripleMean)

neonF <- flag_outliers(neonDat, window_size = 2016)
neonDat <- consolidate_flags(neonF)
neonDat <- remove_outliers(neonDat)

#rm(neonBP, neonTemp)
neonSens <- neon_read(table = "sensor_positions", site = c("KONZ")) 
neonVars <- neon_read(table = "variables", site = c("KONZ"))

konzElev <- neonSens |>  filter(sensorLocationID == "CFGLOC101192") |> distinct() |>  pull(locationReferenceElevation)

kmzElev  <- siteInfo |> 
  filter(siteId == "SFM01") |> 
  pull(Elev_m)
shnElev  <- siteInfo |> 
  filter(siteId == "SHM01") |> 
  pull(Elev_m)

neonBP <- neonDat |> 
  arrange(siteID, dateTime) |>
  mutate(bp_kPa =  zoo::na.approx(bp_kPa, na.rm = F, maxgap = 12),
         airTempC =  zoo::na.approx(airTempC, na.rm = F, maxgap = 12)) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  filter(minute(dateTime) %% 10 == 0) |> 
  select(-siteID, Flag)

kmzNeon <- neonBP |> 
  mutate(Site = "KMZ",
           bp_kPa.cor = correct_bp(bp_kPa, airTempC, konzElev, kmzElev))
shnNeon <- neonBP |> 
  mutate(Site = "SHN",
         bp_kPa.cor = correct_bp(bp_kPa, airTempC, konzElev, shnElev))


bpDat <- ymrBP |> 
  full_join(kmzNeon) |> 
  full_join(shnNeon) |> 
  mutate(bp_mbar = bp_kPa.cor * 10)

bpDat |>
  ggplot(aes(x = dateTime)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = bp_kPa), color = "grey", size = 0.1, alpha = 0.5) +  
  geom_point(aes(y = bp_kPa.cor), color = "#FF0000", size = 0.1, alpha = 0.1) +  
  scale_y_continuous(name = "BP") +
  theme_few() +
  scale_x_datetime(labels = date_format("%b '%y")) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free")
```

# Merge DO and BP

```{r}
selSites <- siteInfo |> 
  filter(Watershed %in% c("SHN", "KMZ", "YMR")) |> 
  select(Site = Watershed, Lat, Long, Elev_m)

doDat <- gpDO |> 
  full_join(bpDat, by = c("Site", "dateTime")) #|> 
  #mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~na.approx(., maxgap = 6, na.rm = F)))


doDat <- doDat |> 
  left_join(selSites) |>
  group_by(Site) |>
  mutate(solar.time = convert_UTC_to_solartime(dateTime, longitude = Long, time.type = "mean solar"),
         isDaytime = suppressWarnings(calc_is_daytime(solar.time, Lat)),
         DO.sat = calc_DO_sat(temp.water = temp.water, bp_mbar, model = "garcia-benson"),
         DO.pctsat = 100 * (DO.obs/ DO.sat),
         WaterYear = calcWaterYear(dateTime)) |> 
  select(-Lat, -Long, -Elev_m)
```

```{r}

doDat |>
  ggplot(aes(dateTime, DO.pctsat)) +
  geom_line(color = "#5d782e") + 
  ylab("DO (% Sat)") +
  theme_few() +
  scale_x_datetime(labels = date_format("%b '%y")) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))+
  facet_wrap(~Site, ncol = 2, scales = "free")

doDat |> 
  filter(temp.water > 0) |> 
  select(dateTime, Site, starts_with('DO')) |>
  gather(type, DO.value, starts_with('DO')) |>
  mutate(units = ifelse(type == 'DO.pctsat', 'DO\n(% sat)', 'DO\n(mg/L)')) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = dateTime, y = DO.value, color = type)) + 
  geom_point(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.1) + 
  facet_grid(units ~ Site, scale = 'free') + 
  theme_few() +
  labs(x = "", y = "")+
  scale_color_manual(name = "variable",
                     values = c("DO.sat" = "#f58231",
                                "DO.pctsat" = "#0082c8",
                                "DO.obs" = "#3cb44b"),
                     labels = c("DO.pctsat" = "DO (% Sat)",
                                "DO.obs" = "DO (Obs)",
                                "DO.sat" = "DO (Sat)"))+
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, alpha = 1)),
         shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))

t <- doDat |> 
  full_join(gpQ, by = c("Site", "dateTime")) |> 
  drop_na(wetQf) |> 
  mutate(wetQf = case_when(
    #(DO.pctsat < 30 & wetQf == "dry") ~ "pooled",
    #(wetQf == "pooled" & DO.pctsat > 95 & Site != "YMR" ~ "dry"),
    wetQf == "dry" & DO.pctsat < 60 & Site != "KMZ" ~ "pooled",
    TRUE ~ wetQf  # Keep NA values as NA
  ))

# Define color mapping for wetQf categories
wetQf_colors <- c("flowing" = "#0082c8", 
                  "pooled"  = "#3cb44b",  
                  "dry"     = "#f58231")  

# Loop through each site
for (site in unique(t$Site)) {
  # Filter data for the current site
  df <- t |>
    filter(Site == site)
  
  # Get unique wetQf levels and match them to colors
  unique_wetQf <- unique(df$wetQf)
  color_vector <- unname(wetQf_colors[unique_wetQf])  # Remove names for dygraph compatibility

  # Create xts objects for DO.pctsat (split by wetQf)
  do_xts_list <- lapply(unique_wetQf, function(wetQf_level) {
    df_filtered <- df |> filter(wetQf == wetQf_level)
    xts::xts(df_filtered$DO.pctsat, order.by = df_filtered$dateTime)
  })
  names(do_xts_list) <- unique_wetQf  # Ensure names match wetQf levels
  
  # Create xts object for swDepth (not split by wetQf)
  depth_xts <- xts::xts(df$swDepth, order.by = df$dateTime)
  names(depth_xts) <- "swDepth"
  
  # Combine DO and swDepth into a single xts object
  combined_xts <- do.call(cbind, c(do_xts_list, list(depth_xts)))

  # Create dygraph
  dygraph(combined_xts, main = paste("Site:", site, "DO Sat and Water Depth")) |>
    dyAxis("y", label = "DO Sat (%)") |>
    dyAxis("y2", label = "Water Depth (m)", independentTicks = TRUE) |>
    dySeries("swDepth", axis = "y2") |>  # Assign swDepth to the second y-axis
    dyRangeSelector() |>
    dyOptions(drawGrid = FALSE, useDataTimezone = TRUE, connectSeparatedPoints = FALSE, colors = color_vector) |> 
    dyLegend(show = "always") |>
    print()
}

```

# Calc DO Metrics

```{r}
#dailyDOtest <- daily_summary(doDat)
# Daily metrics
dailyDO <- doDat |> 
  #filter(wetQf != "Dry") |> 
  mutate(Date = as_date(dateTime)) |>
  group_by(Site, Date) |>
  summarize(
    minDO.obs = min(DO.obs, na.rm = T),
    maxDO.obs = max(DO.obs, na.rm = T),
    minDO.pctsat = min(DO.pctsat, na.rm = T),
    maxDO.pctsat = max(DO.pctsat, na.rm = T),
    avgDO.obs = mean(DO.obs, na.rm = T),
    avgDO.pctsat = mean(DO.pctsat, na.rm = T),
    DOSat.hyp = sum(DO.pctsat <= 50, na.rm = T),
    DO.hyp = sum(DO.obs <= 2, na.rm = T),
    totalDO = n()
    ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.finite(.), ., NA))) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(if_any(everything(), is.na), NA, .))) |> 
  mutate(across(minDO.obs:avgDO.pctsat, ~ ifelse(. == 0, NA, .))) |> 
  mutate(DO.amp = maxDO.obs - minDO.obs,
         DOpctsat.amp = maxDO.pctsat - minDO.pctsat,
         probHyp = DO.hyp / totalDO,
         probHypSat = DOSat.hyp / totalDO)
#nighttime hypoxia ratio
nightHypRat <- doDat |> 
  #filter(wetQf != "Dry") |> 
  mutate(across(c("DO.obs", "DO.pctsat", "DO.sat"), ~ ifelse(. == 0, NA, .))) |> 
  select(Site, dateTime, solar.time, DO.obs, DO.pctsat, DO.sat, isDaytime) |> 
  mutate(Date = as_date(dateTime)) |>
  group_by(Date, Site, isDaytime) |> 
  summarise(DO.hyp.obs = sum(DO.obs <= 2, na.rm = TRUE),
            DO.hyp.sat = sum(DO.pctsat <= 50, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') |> 
  pivot_wider(names_from = isDaytime, 
              values_from = c(DO.hyp.obs, DO.hyp.sat), 
              names_prefix = "DO_hyp_", 
              names_sep = "_") |> 
  rename(DayHyp_obs = DO.hyp.obs_DO_hyp_TRUE, 
         NightHyp_obs = DO.hyp.obs_DO_hyp_FALSE,
         DayHyp_sat = DO.hyp.sat_DO_hyp_TRUE, 
         NightHyp_sat = DO.hyp.sat_DO_hyp_FALSE) |> 
  mutate(NHR_obs = NightHyp_obs / DayHyp_obs,
         NHR_sat = NightHyp_sat / DayHyp_sat) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.finite(.), ., NA))) |> 
  select(Date, Site, NHR_obs, NHR_sat)


# Calculate probabilities for daytime
dayHypProb <- doDat |> 
  #filter(wetQf != "Dry") |> 
  mutate(across(c("DO.obs", "DO.pctsat", "DO.sat"), ~ ifelse(. == 0, NA, .))) |> 
  select(Site, dateTime, solar.time, DO.obs, DO.pctsat, DO.sat, isDaytime) |> 
  mutate(Date = as_date(dateTime)) |>
  filter(isDaytime) |>
  group_by(Site, Date) |>
  summarise(
    dayProbConc = mean(DO.obs < 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    dayProbSat = mean(DO.pctsat < 50, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Calculate probabilities for nighttime
nightHypProb <- doDat |>
  #filter(wetQf != "Dry") |> 
  mutate(across(c("DO.obs", "DO.pctsat", "DO.sat"), ~ ifelse(. == 0, NA, .))) |> 
  select(Site, dateTime, solar.time, DO.obs, DO.pctsat, DO.sat, isDaytime) |> 
  mutate(Date = as_date(dateTime)) |>
  filter(!isDaytime) |>
  group_by(Site, Date) |>
  summarise(
    nightProbConc = mean(DO.obs < 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    nightProbSat = mean(DO.pctsat < 50, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Join the daytime and nighttime probabilities
nightHypRat <- dayHypProb |>
  inner_join(nightHypProb, by = c("Site", "Date")) |> 
  mutate(NHR_obs = nightProbConc/dayProbConc,
         NHR_sat = nightProbSat/dayProbSat) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.finite(.), ., NA)))

#Merge DO Metrics
doMetrics <- dailyDO |> 
  full_join(nightHypRat) |> 
  left_join(flowState, by = c("Site", "Date")) |> 
  mutate(WaterYear = calcWaterYear(Date),
         season = sapply(Date, get_season),
         Year = year(Date)) |> 
  arrange(Site, Date, WaterYear)


doMetrics |>
  ggplot(aes(Date, avgDO.pctsat, color = flowState)) +
  geom_point() +
  ylab("DO (mg/L)") +
  theme_few() +
  scale_x_date(labels = date_format("%b '%y")) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))+
  facet_wrap(~Site, ncol = 2, scales = "free")
```

## DO Plots

```{r}
doMetrics <- doMetrics |> 
  #filter(flowState != "Dry") |> 
  filter(avgDO.pctsat != 0) |> 
  mutate(Year = factor(Year),
         season = factor(season, levels = c("Spring", "Summer", "Fall", "Winter"), ordered = T))

#doMet |> 
#  write_csv("~/Desktop/DOMetrics.csv")

doMetrics |>
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = Site, y = avgDO.pctsat, color = flowState)) +  # Add color = Site
  geom_boxplot(outlier.color = NA) +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.75), alpha = 0.5) +
  #scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +  # Add this line
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "", y = expression(Daily ~ DO["%Sat"])) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom")

doMetrics |>
  #filter(!Site == "SHN") |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = avgDO.pctsat, fill = flowState, after_stat(scaled))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +  # Add this line
  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("KMZ" = "#127088", "YMR" = "#DE3C37"))+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = expression(DO["%sat"]), y = "Density") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())+
  facet_wrap(~Site)

doMetrics |>
  #filter(!Site == "SHN") |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = avgDO.pctsat, fill = Site, after_stat(scaled))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +  # Add this line
  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("KMZ" = "#127088", "YMR" = "#DE3C37"))+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = expression(DO["%sat"]), y = "Density") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())+
  facet_wrap(~Site)

doMetrics |>
  #filter(season %in% c("Spring", "Summer")) |> 
  #filter(!Site == "SHN") |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(Site, DOpctsat.amp, color = flowState)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.75), alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_few() +
  labs(y = expression(Daily ~ DO["%Sat"] ~ "Amplitude"))+
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank()) 

doMetrics |>
  #filter(season %in% c("Spring", "Summer")) |> 
  #filter(!Site == "SHN") |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(Site, DOpctsat.amp, color = flowState)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.75), alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_few() +
  labs(y = expression(Daily ~ DO["%Sat"] ~ "Amplitude"))+
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank()) +
  facet_wrap(~Year)

doMetrics |>
  #filter(!Site == "SHN") |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = DOpctsat.amp, fill = Site, after_stat(scaled))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +  # Add this line
  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("KMZ" = "#127088", "YMR" = "#DE3C37"))+
  labs(x = expression(Daily ~ DO["%Sat"] ~ "Amplitude"), y = "Density") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())+
  facet_wrap(~flowState, ncol = 1)

doMetrics |>
  filter(season %in% c("Spring", "Summer")) |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(Site, DOpctsat.amp, color = season)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.75), alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_few() +
  labs(y = expression(Daily ~ DO["%Sat"] ~ "Amplitude"))+ 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Spring" = "#275E4D", "Summer" = "#FEB424", "Winter" = "#1073d6", "Fall" = "#743203"))+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  facet_wrap(~flowState, scales = "fixed", ncol = 1) 

doMetrics |>
  filter(season %in% c("Spring", "Summer")) |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(Site, avgDO.pctsat, color = season)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.75), alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_few() +
  labs(y = expression(Daily ~ DO["%Sat"]))+ 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Spring" = "#275E4D", "Summer" = "#FEB424", "Winter" = "#1073d6", "Fall" = "#743203"))+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  facet_wrap(~flowState, scales = "fixed", ncol = 1) 


doMetrics |>
  #filter(!Site == "SHN") |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = avgDO.pctsat, fill = Site, after_stat(scaled))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.8) +
  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("KMZ" = "#127088", "YMR" = "#DE3C37"))+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = expression(DO["%sat"]), y = "Density") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())+
  facet_wrap(~flowState, ncol = 1)

doMetrics |>
  #filter(!Site == "SHN") |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = avgDO.pctsat, fill = flowState, after_stat(scaled))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.8) +
  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("KMZ" = "#127088", "YMR" = "#DE3C37"))+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = expression(DO["%sat"]), y = "Density") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())+
  facet_wrap(~Site, ncol = 1)

doMetrics |>
  #filter(!Site == "SHN") |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = probHypSat, fill = flowState, after_stat(scaled))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.8) +
  #xlim(-1.5, 1.5)+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(x = expression(DO["%Sat"] ~ "Probability of Hypoxia"), y = "Density") +
  theme_few() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())+
  facet_wrap(~Site, scale = "free_y")

doMetrics |>
  filter(season %in% c("Spring", "Summer")) |> 
  filter(flowState %in% c("flowing", "pooled")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = probHypSat, fill = season, after_stat(scaled))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.8) +
  #xlim(-1.5, 1.5)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Spring" = "#275E4D", "Summer" = "#FEB424", "Winter" = "#1073d6", "Fall" = "#743203"))+
  labs(x = expression(DO["%Sat"] ~ "Probability of Hypoxia"), y = "Density") +
  theme_few() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())+
  facet_wrap(~Site, scale = "free_y")
```

# Discharge

```{r}
dailyQ <- gpQ |>
  filter(swDepth > 0) 
  
setDT(dailyQ)
dailyQ <- dailyQ[, .(
  minQ = min(Q_Ls, na.rm = TRUE),
  maxQ = max(Q_Ls, na.rm = TRUE),
  Q_1d = mean(Q_Ls, na.rm = TRUE),
  minDepth = min(swDepth, na.rm = TRUE),
  maxDepth = max(swDepth, na.rm = TRUE),
  Depth_1d = mean(swDepth, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = .(Site, Date = as.Date(dateTime))]
# Replace NA/NaN in all numeric columns
dailyQ[, names(dailyQ)[sapply(dailyQ, is.numeric)] := lapply(.SD, function(x) fifelse(is.na(x), 0, x)), .SDcols = names(dailyQ)[sapply(dailyQ, is.numeric)]]

# Calculate exceedance probabilities and scale depth
plan(multisession)  # Enable parallelization
dailyQ <- dailyQ |> 
  group_split(Site) |> 
  future_map_dfr(~ .x |> 
                   mutate(across(c(minDepth, maxDepth, Depth_1d), 
                                 ~ bestNormalize(.x)$x.t, 
                                 .names = "{.col}_norm"),  # Normalization
                          across(c(minDepth, maxDepth, Depth_1d), 
                                 ~ rescale(.x),  # Min-max scaling from 0 to 1
                                 .names = "{.col}_scaled"), 
                          excProb = calc_exceedance_prob(Q_1d)),
                 .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE), 
                 .progress = FALSE) |> 
  ungroup()
plan(sequential)


dailyQ |> 
ggplot(aes(x = excProb, y = Q_1d, color = Site)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    x = "Exceedance Probability (%)",
    y = "Flow (L/s)",
    title = "Flow Exceedance Probability by Site"
  ) +
  theme_few()

  select(Site, Depth_1d_scaled, Depth_1d_norm) |>  # Select relevant columns
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Depth_1d_scaled, Depth_1d_norm), 
               names_to = "Type", 
               values_to = "Depth_1d_Value") |> 
ggplot(aes(x = Depth_1d_Value, fill = Type)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, bins = 30, position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Depth_1d_scaled" = "red", "Depth_1d_norm" = "green")) +  # Different colors
  theme_few() +
  facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free_x") +  # Free scales for different sites
  labs(title = "Comparison of Scaled vs Unscaled Depth Data",
       x = "Depth Value",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Data Type")
  
dailyQ <- dailyQ |> 
  select(Site, Date, minQ, maxQ, Q_1d, minDepth_scaled, maxDepth_scaled, Depth_1d_scaled, excProb)
```

# PPT

```{r}
# Howard 14NW Met Station
ymrPPT <- list.files("~/Dropbox/AIMS/Data/MetData/YMR Met Station", full.names = T)
ymrPPT <- sapply(ymrPPT, fread, check.names = T, simplify = FALSE) |> 
  bind_rows(.id = "id") |> 
  select(dateTime = TIMESTAMP, ppt_mm = PRECIP) |> 
  mutate(Site = "YMR") |> 
  mutate(Date = as.Date(dateTime)) |> 
  group_by(Site, Date) |>                                
  summarize(
    ppt_mm = sum(ppt_mm)    
  ) |> 
  mutate(Source = "KS Mesonet")


lterPPT <- read_csv("~/Dropbox/AIMS/Data/LTER/PPT/lterPPT.csv") |> 
  mutate(Site = "KMZ") |> 
  bind_rows(read_csv("~/Dropbox/AIMS/Data/LTER/PPT/lterPPT.csv") |> 
              mutate(Site = "SHN")) |> 
  filter(Watershed == "HQ") |> 
  select(Date = RecDate, Site, ppt_mm = PPT_mm) |> 
  mutate(Source = "LTER")

noaaStations <- rnoaa::ghcnd_stations() |> 
  group_by(id, latitude, longitude, elevation, state, name, gsn_flag, wmo_id, first_year, last_year) |> 
  summarise(
    elements = paste(unique(element), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop") 


# Apply to all sites
closest_stations <- siteInfo  |> 
  filter(Watershed %in% c("SHN", "KMZ")) |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(closest_station = list(find_closest_station(pick(everything()), noaaStations)))  |> 
  unnest_wider(closest_station)

noaaPPT <- rnoaa::meteo_pull_monitors(monitors = closest_stations$id,
                                      date_min = "2021-01-01",
                                      date_max = today(),
                                      var = "prcp")  |> 
  left_join(closest_stations |> select(id, Watershed), by = "id")  |>  
  select(Site = Watershed, Date = date, ppt_mm = prcp) |> 
  mutate(ppt_mm = ppt_mm/10, #tenths of mm to mm
         #Temp_C = ((tmin+tmax)/2.0)/10,
         Source = "NOAA")

knzPPT <- noaaPPT |> 
  select(-Source, Site, Date, ppt_noaa = ppt_mm) |> 
  full_join(lterPPT |> select(-Source, Site, Date, ppt_lter = ppt_mm)) |> 
  mutate(ppt_mm = coalesce(ppt_lter, ppt_noaa)) |> 
  filter(Date >= "2021-01-01") |> 
  select(Site, Date, ppt_mm)

  

allPPT <- ymrPPT |> 
  full_join(lterPPT) |> 
  full_join(noaaPPT) |> 
  filter(Date >= "2021-01-01") 


dailyPPT <- ymrPPT |> 
  select(-Source) |> 
  full_join(knzPPT) |>
  rename(PPT_1d = ppt_mm)

# Define rolling window sizes
window_sizes <- c(7, 14, 30, 60)

setDT(dailyPPT)
dailyPPT[, (paste0("PPT_", window_sizes, "d")) := 
              lapply(window_sizes, function(w) frollsum(get("PPT_1d"), n = w, align = "right", na.rm = TRUE)),
           by = Site]
```

## STICs

```{r}
kmzStics <- here("Data/STICs/KNZ_STIC_combined")
kmzStics <- list.files(kmzStics, "*.csv", full.names = T, recursive = T)
kmzStics <- sapply(kmzStics, fread, simplify = F) |> 
  bind_rows() |> 
  mutate(Site = "KMZ") |> 
  select(-project, -rType, -SN, -rep) |> 
  rename(dateTime = datetime)


ymrStics <- here("Data/STICs/YMR_STIC_combined/")
ymrStics <- list.files(ymrStics, "*.csv", full.names = T, recursive = T)
ymrStics <- sapply(ymrStics, fread, simplify = F) |> 
  bind_rows() |> 
   mutate(Site = "YMR") |> 
  select(-project, -rType, -SN, -rep) |> 
  rename(dateTime = datetime)

shnStics <- here("Data/STICs/SHN_STIC_combined/")
shnStics <- list.files(shnStics, "*.csv", full.names = T, recursive = T)
shnStics <- sapply(shnStics, fread, simplify = F) |> 
  bind_rows() |> 
   mutate(Site = "SHN") |> 
  select(-project, -rType, -SN, -rep) |> 
  rename(dateTime = datetime) |> 
    mutate(wetdry = ifelse(wetdry == 0, "dry", "wet"))

sticDat <- kmzStics |> 
  full_join(ymrStics) |> 
  full_join(shnStics) |> 
  group_by(siteID) |> 
  complete(dateTime = seq(floor_date(min(dateTime), "15 min"), floor_date(max(dateTime), "15 min"), by = "15 min")) |> 
  arrange(dateTime) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  left_join(selSites) |> 
  mutate(solar.time = convert_UTC_to_solartime(dateTime, longitude = Long, time.type = "mean solar"))

dailyPercWet <- sticDat |>
  mutate(Date = as.Date(dateTime)) |> 
  group_by(Site, Date) |>                                
  summarize(
    PercWet_1d = (sum(wetdry == "wet", na.rm = T) / n()) * 100) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  group_by(Site) |> 
  mutate(dPercWet_1d = PercWet_1d - lag(PercWet_1d, default = NA)) |> 
  ungroup()


```

```{r}
# Remove unnecessary columns from doMet
setDT(doMetrics)[, c("WaterYear", "season", "Year", "totalDO") := NULL]

# Convert to data.tables and set keys before merging
datasets <- list(doMetrics, dailyQ, dailyPPT, dailyPercWet)
datasets <- lapply(datasets, as.data.table)  # Ensure all are data.tables

# Set keys for efficient merging
lapply(datasets, setkey, Site, Date)

# Perform iterative merge with Reduce
dailyDat <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = c("Site", "Date"), all = TRUE), datasets)

# Remove rows with NA Site
dailyDat <- dailyDat[!is.na(Site)]

# Define rolling window sizes
window_sizes <- c(7, 14, 30, 60)

# List of variables to compute rolling means for
vars <- list(
  Q_1d = "Q", 
  Depth_1d_scaled = "Depth",
  PercWet_1d = "PercWet",
  dPercWet_1d = "dPercWet"
)

# Generate rolling means for each variable and window size
for (var in names(vars)) {
  prefix <- vars[[var]]  # Get the appropriate prefix for the variable
  dailyDat[, (paste0(prefix, "_", window_sizes, "d")) := 
              lapply(window_sizes, function(w) frollmean(get(var), n = w, align = "right", na.rm = TRUE)),
           by = Site]
}

# remove rows without DO Metrics and save to csv
dailyDat <- dailyDat |> 
  drop_na(avgDO.pctsat) |> 
  write_csv(here("Data/dailyData.csv"))

# Gather the rolling mean data into a long format
rollingCols <- grep("_(\\d+d)$", names(dailyDat), value = TRUE)  # Select columns like Q_7d, Depth_7d, etc.
longDat <- melt(dailyDat, id.vars = c("Site", "Date"), measure.vars = rollingCols, 
                variable.name = "Variable", value.name = "Value")

# Extract the variable type (e.g., Q, Depth, etc.) and rolling window size (e.g., 7d, 14d)
longDat[, c("VariableType", "Window") := tstrsplit(Variable, "_", fixed = TRUE)]

# List to store plots
plots <- list()

longDat <- longDat |> 
  group_by(Site, VariableType, Window) |> 
  complete(Date = seq(min(Date, na.rm = TRUE), max(Date, na.rm = TRUE), by = "1 day")) |> 
  ungroup()

# Create separate plots for each VariableType
unique_vars <- unique(longDat$VariableType)

for (var in unique_vars) {
  p <- longDat |> 
    filter(VariableType == var) |>  # Use `filter()` instead of `[ ]`
    ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = Value, color = Window, group = Window)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", na.rm = T) +  
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("1d" = "grey", "7d" = "blue", "14d" = "green", "30d" = "orange", "60d" = "red"),
      name = "Rolling Window"
    ) +
    labs(x = "Date", y = var) +
    theme_few() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10)) +
    facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1)

  # Store the plot
  plots[[var]] <- p
}

# Display plots
for (p in plots) {
  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
dailyDat |>
  ggplot(aes(x = excProb, y = Q_1d, color = Site)) +
  geom_line(na.rm = T) +
  labs(x = "Exceedance Probability (%)", y = "Flow (L/s)", title = "Flow Exceedance Probability by Site") +
  theme_few()

dailyDat |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = Depth_1d_scaled, color = Site)) +
  geom_line(na.rm = T) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Depth (m)", ) +
  theme_few()
```

## Cor Plot

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(PerformanceAnalytics)
library(corrr)

dailyDat |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !ends_with("_scaled"), 
                ~ scale(.x) |> as.vector())) |>  # Save scaled version as *_std
  pivot_longer(cols = where(is.numeric), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = Value)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "#3cb44b", bins = 30) +
  facet_wrap(~Variable, scales = "free") +
  theme_few() +
  labs(title = "Histograms of Center & Scaled Variables", x = "Value", y = "Frequency")


dailyDat |>
  pivot_longer(cols = where(is.numeric), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = Value)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "#3cb44b") +
  facet_wrap(~Variable, scales = "free") +
  theme_few() +
  labs(title = "Histograms of Variables", x = "Value", y = "Frequency")

dailyDat |>
  select(where(is.numeric)) |>
  scale(center = T, scale = T) |> 
  chart.Correlation(histogram = T, method = "pearson")

dailyDat |>
  select(where(is.numeric)) |>
  scale(center = T, scale = T) |> 
  cor(method = "pearson", use = "complete.obs") |> 
  corrplot(type = "upper")

dailyDatCorr <- dailyDat |>
  select(where(is.numeric)) |>
  correlate()
dailyDatCorr |> 
  rearrange(absolute = FALSE) |> 
  shave(upper = T) |>
  rplot(print_cor = T)

dailyDatCorr |> 
  network_plot()

ggplot(dailyDat, aes(Depth_1d_scaled, excProb)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_few() +
  facet_wrap(~Site)
ggplot(dailyDat, aes(Q_1d)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_few() +
  facet_wrap(~Site)
ggplot(dailyDat, aes(excProb)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_few() +
  facet_wrap(~Site)
ggplot(dailyDat, aes(Depth_1d_scaled)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_few() +
  facet_wrap(~Site)
```

# SEM

```{r}
library(lavaan)

# dailyDat |> 
#   ggplot(aes(x = Depth_7d , y = avgDO.pctsat, color = Site)) +
#   geom_point() +
#   theme_few()

# Define the SEM model as a string
sem_model <- '  
  # Regressions for DO
  minDO.pctsat ~ PercWet_7d + Q_7d + Depth_7d + excProb

  maxDO.pctsat ~ PercWet_7d + Q_7d + Depth_7d + excProb

  avgDO.pctsat ~ PercWet_7d + Q_7d + Depth_7d + excProb
  

  # Regressions for Q, Depth, and PercWet
  PercWet_7d ~ PPT_7d + Q_7d
  Q_7d ~ PPT_7d + Depth_7d
  
  # Covariates
  #minDO.pctsat ~~ maxDO.pctsat + avgDO.pctsat
  Q_7d ~~ Depth_7d
'

# Fit the SEM model using a hypothetical dataset 'data'
fit <- sem(sem_model, data = dailyDat)

# Summarize the results
summary(fit, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)
library(semPlot)
semPaths(fit, "std", "est", style = "lisrel")
semPaths(fit, "Standardized", "Estimates")
```

```{r}
library(lavaan)
library(dplyr)
library(doParallel)
library(foreach)

# List of variables for each component
PPT_vars <- c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")
Depth_vars <- c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
Q_vars <- c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d")
PercWet_vars <- c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d")

# Generate all combinations of variables
combinations <- expand.grid(PPT = PPT_vars, Depth = Depth_vars, Q = Q_vars, PercWet = PercWet_vars)

# Set up parallel backend
num_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1 # Use one less than the total cores
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Parallel loop to fit SEM models
results <- foreach(i = 1:nrow(combinations), .packages = c("lavaan", "dplyr")) %dopar% {
  # Extract current combination
  current_combo <- combinations[i, ]
  
  # Define the SEM model dynamically
  sem_model <- paste0('  
    # Regressions for DO
    minDO.pctsat ~ ', current_combo$PercWet, ' + ', current_combo$Q, ' + ', current_combo$Depth, ' + excProb

    maxDO.pctsat ~ ', current_combo$PercWet, ' + ', current_combo$Q, ' + ', current_combo$Depth, ' + excProb

    avgDO.pctsat ~ ', current_combo$PercWet, ' + ', current_combo$Q, ' + ', current_combo$Depth, ' + excProb

    # Regressions for Q, Depth, and PercWet
    ', current_combo$PercWet, ' ~ ', current_combo$PPT, ' + ', current_combo$Q, '
    ', current_combo$Q, ' ~ ', current_combo$PPT, ' + ', current_combo$Depth, '
  ')
  
  # Fit the SEM model
  fit <- sem(sem_model, data = dailyDat)
  
  # Extract key fit measures
  fit_measures <- fitMeasures(fit, c("aic", "bic", "rmsea", "cfi"))
  
  # Return the results for the current combination
  list(
    combo = current_combo,
    fit_measures = fit_measures
  )
}

# Stop the parallel cluster
stopCluster(cl)

# Combine results into a data frame
fit_measures <- do.call(rbind, lapply(results, function(x) {
  data.frame(
    Combination = paste(x$combo, collapse = ", "),
    AIC = x$fit_measures["aic"],
    BIC = x$fit_measures["bic"],
    RMSEA = x$fit_measures["rmsea"],
    CFI = x$fit_measures["cfi"]
  )
}))

# View the fit measures
print(fit_measures)

```

```{r}
siteColors <- c('#1b9e77','#d95f02','#7570b3')
siteShapes <- calc_shape_pal()(length(unique(dailyDat$Site)))
```

# PCA

```{r}
metClean <- dailyDat |>
  select(Site, Date,
         minDO.pctsat, maxDO.pctsat, avgDO.pctsat, DOpctsat.amp) |> 
  drop_na()

#PCA
# Select columns for PCA
metPca <- metClean |>
  select(
    minDO.pctsat, maxDO.pctsat, avgDO.pctsat, DOpctsat.amp
  ) |>
  drop_na() |>  # Remove rows with missing values
  scale(center = T, scale = T) |> 
  prcomp() # do PCA on scaled data

#extract loadings
metPca |>
  tidy(matrix = "loadings")

#Variance explained
metPca |>
  tidy(matrix = "eigenvalues")

#Scree plot
fviz_eig(metPca, addlabels = TRUE)

#Qual of representation
fviz_cos2(metPca, choice = "var", axes = 1) -> pc1plt
pc1plt
#ggsave(filename = "doPC1.png", plot = pc1plt, path = "~/Desktop/lmFigs", device = "png", width = 21, height = 15, units = "cm", dpi = "retina")
fviz_cos2(metPca, choice = "var", axes = 2) -> pc2plt
pc2plt
#ggsave(filename = "doPC2.png", plot = pc2plt, path = "~/Desktop/lmFigs", device = "png", width = 21, height = 15, units = "cm", dpi = "retina")
fviz_cos2(metPca, choice = "var", axes = 3)

#Biplot
doPCA <- fviz_pca_biplot(metPca, geom = "point", habillage = metClean$Site,
                         addEllipses = TRUE, 
                         ellipse.level = 0.95,
                         col.var = "black", 
                         repel = TRUE)+
  scale_shape_cleveland()+
  #scale_shape_manual(values = siteShapes) + # Custom shapes
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  theme_few()
doPCA
#ggsave(filename = "doPCA.png", plot = doPCA, path = "~/Desktop/lmFigs", device = "png", width = 21, height = 15, units = "cm", dpi = "retina")

  
fviz_pca_var(metPca, 
  addEllipses = F, 
  repel = TRUE)+
  theme_few()

# Compute PCA Scores for PC1 and PC2
pcaScores <- as.data.frame(metPca$x) |>
  select(PC1, PC2) |>  # Extract only PC1 and PC2
  rownames_to_column(var = "row_id") |>  # Preserve row IDs
  mutate(row_id = as.integer(row_id))  # Convert row_id to integer

# Add PC scores to metClean
metCleanWithPC <- metClean |>
  mutate(row_id = row_number()) |>  # Add a row ID for matching
  left_join(pcaScores, by = "row_id") |>  # Merge with PCA scores
  select(-row_id)  # Remove the temporary row ID column

# Add the PC scores back to dailyDatRoll
dailyDat <- dailyDat |>
  left_join(metCleanWithPC |>
              select(Site, Date, PC1, PC2),
            by = c("Site", "Date"))

```

```{r}
library(cluster)
library(factoextra)

# Select DO columns and drop missing values
do_data <- dailyDat |>
  select(Site, minDO.pctsat, maxDO.pctsat, avgDO.pctsat, DOpctsat.amp) |> 
  drop_na() |> 
  mutate(Site = as.factor(Site))  # Convert Site to factor
# Scale the data
do_scaled <- do_data |> 
  select(-Site) |> 
  scale(center = T, scale = T) |> 
  as_tibble()

# optimal number of clusters using the elbow method
fviz_nbclust(do_scaled, kmeans, method = "wss") +
  labs(title = "Elbow Method for Optimal Clusters")

fviz_nbclust(do_scaled, kmeans, method = "silhouette") +
  labs(title = "Silhouette Method for Optimal Clusters")

# k-means clustering
set.seed(123) # For reproducibility
kmeans_result <- eclust(do_scaled, 
                        FUNcluster = "kmeans",  # Use K-means clustering
                        k = 3,                 # Number of clusters
                        nstart = 25,           # Number of random starts for K-means
                        graph = F) 

# Visualize clusters
fviz_cluster(kmeans_result, geom = "point", ggtheme = theme_few())

# Create a cluster column
do_data$Clusters <- as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster)
# Perform PCA
kmeansPCA <- do_scaled |> 
  prcomp(center = F, scale. = F) #already scaled

fviz_pca_biplot(kmeansPCA, geom = "point", habillage = do_data$Site,
                         addEllipses = TRUE, 
                         ellipse.level = 0.95,
                         col.var = "black", 
                         repel = TRUE)+
  scale_shape_cleveland()+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  theme_few()

# Add PCA coordinates to the original data
kmeansPCA_scores <- as_tibble(kmeansPCA$x)
kmeansPCA_scores <- bind_cols(do_data, kmeansPCA_scores)

# Extract PCA loadings
kmeansPCA_loadings <- as.data.frame(kmeansPCA$rotation) |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "Variable") |> 
  mutate(PC1 = PC1 * max(abs(kmeansPCA_scores$PC1)),  # Scale arrows
         PC2 = PC2 * max(abs(kmeansPCA_scores$PC2)))
#extract loadings
metPca |>
  tidy(matrix = "loadings")

#Variance explained
metPca |>
  tidy(matrix = "eigenvalues")
# ggplot-based PCA Visualization with Site shapes
ggplot(kmeansPCA_scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Clusters, shape = Site)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +  # Points with site-based shapes
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Clusters, color = Clusters), level = 0.95) + # Ellipses for clusters
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +  # Color scheme for clusters
  scale_shape_manual(values = 1:length(unique(kmeansPCA_scores$Site))) +  # Unique shapes per site
  geom_segment(data = kmeansPCA_loadings, aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm")), color = "black", inherit.aes = F) +  # Arrows for variables
  geom_text(data = kmeansPCA_loadings, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, label = Variable),
            vjust = -0.5, color = "black", fontface = "bold", inherit.aes = F)+  # Labels for variables
  labs(title = "PCA - Clustered by K-Means with Site Shapes",
       x = paste0("Dim1 (", round(summary(kmeansPCA)$importance[2, 1] * 100, 1), "%)"),
       y = paste0("Dim2 (", round(summary(kmeansPCA)$importance[2, 2] * 100, 1), "%)"),
       color = "Cluster",
       shape = "Site") +
  theme_few()


dailyDat <- dailyDat |>
  drop_na(avgDO.pctsat) |>
  mutate(cluster = wcke$cluster) #|> 
  #write_csv("~/Desktop/dailyDat.csv")

pca_result <- prcomp(do_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# Add PCA scores and cluster info to a data frame
pca_data <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x) |>
  mutate(
    Site = do_data$Site,       # Add Site back to data
    Cluster = as.factor(wcke$cluster) # Add cluster info
  )

# Step 6: Plot PCA colored by Cluster and shaped by Site
ggplot(pca_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Cluster, shape = Site)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "PC 1 (68%)",
    y = "PC 2 (32%)",
    color = "Cluster",
    shape = "Site"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  theme_few() +
  scale_shape_cleveland()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

explained_variance <- pca_result$sdev^2 / sum(pca_result$sdev^2)

# Create a table with percent explained for each PC
percent_variance <- explained_variance * 100

# Combine into a table for easy viewing
variance_table <- data.frame(
  PC = paste0("PC", 1:length(percent_variance)),
  Variance_Explained = percent_variance
)

# Print the table
print(variance_table)


```

```{r}

# Define custom colors
custom_colors <- c("1" = "#228833", "2" = "#66CCEE", "3" = "#AA3377")

# Create the plot
ggplot(dailyDat, aes(x = Date, y = avgDO.pctsat, color = factor(cluster))) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, name = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Average DO % Saturation"
  ) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# Daily Min
ggplot(dailyDat, aes(x = Date, y = minDO.pctsat, color = factor(cluster))) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, name = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Minimum DO % Saturation"
  ) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

ggplot(dailyDat, aes(x = Date, y = maxDO.pctsat, color = factor(cluster))) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, name = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Max DO % Saturation"
  ) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )


# Create month column and calculate frequencies
cluster_freq <- dailyDat |>
  mutate(Month = factor(month(Date, label = TRUE))) |>
  group_by(Site, Month, cluster) |>
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop') |>
  mutate(cluster = factor(cluster))

# Create the plot
ggplot(cluster_freq, aes(x = Month, y = count, fill = factor(cluster))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, name = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cluster Frequency by Month and Site",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

ggplot(cluster_freq, aes(x = Month, y = count, fill = factor(cluster))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, name = "Cluster") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cluster Distribution by Month and Site",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Proportion"
  ) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

```

```{r}

# Specify the variables for which to create boxplots
vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat",
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "dPercWet_1d", "dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"
)

# Define custom colors for clusters
custom_colors <- c("1" = "#228833", "2" = "#66CCEE", "3" = "#AA3377")

pdf(here("Figs/cluster/Boxplots_by_Cluster.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
# Loop through variables and create boxplots
for (var in vars) {
  # Check if the variable exists in the dataframe
  if (var %in% names(dailyDat)) {
    # Create the boxplot
    p <- ggplot(dailyDat, aes_string(x = "factor(cluster)", y = var, fill = "factor(cluster)")) +
      geom_boxplot() +
        scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Boxplot of", var, "by Cluster"),
        x = "Cluster",
        y = var,
        fill = "Cluster"
      ) +
      theme_few()
    
    # Add the plot to the PDF
    print(p) # Ensure that this writes to the PDF device
  } else {
    message(paste("Variable", var, "does not exist in the dataframe."))
  }
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()
```

```{r}
# Specify the variables for which to create histograms
vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat",
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "dPercWet_1d", "dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"
)

pdf(here("Figs/cluster/Histograms_by_Cluster.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
# Create grouped bar plot for Site counts by cluster
grouped_histogram <- ggplot(dailyDat, aes(x = Site, fill = factor(cluster))) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Site Counts by Cluster",
    x = "Site",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Cluster"
  ) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
print(grouped_histogram)

# Loop through variables and create histograms
for (var in vars) {
  # Check if the variable exists in the dataframe
  if (var %in% names(dailyDat)) {
    # Create the histogram
    p <- ggplot(dailyDat, aes_string(x = var, fill = "factor(cluster)")) +
      geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, position = "identity") +
        scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Histogram of", var),
        x = var,
        y = "Count",
        fill = "Cluster"
      ) +
      theme_few()

    # Add the plot to the PDF
    print(p) # Ensure that this writes to the PDF device
  } else {
    message(paste("Variable", var, "does not exist in the dataframe."))
  }
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()
```

## Rand Forest

```{r}
library(ranger)
response_vars <- c("Site", "avgDO.pctsat")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "dPercWet_1d", "dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"
)


rfDat <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) 
# Split data into training and testing sets
set.seed(123)
datSplit <- initial_split(rfDat, prop = 0.75)
datTrain <- training(datSplit)
datTest <- testing(datSplit)

#Set up model
rfMod <- 
  rand_forest() |> 
  set_engine("ranger",
             num.threads = (parallel::detectCores() - 1),
             importance = "permutation") |> 
  set_mode("regression")

#Preprocessing 
clustRecipe <- datTrain |> 
  recipe(avgDO.pctsat ~ .) |> #predict gpp based on all predictors
  update_role(Site, new_role = "ID") |> #set ID vars and dont use in model
  step_naomit(everything(), skip = TRUE) |> 
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) |> 
  step_corr(all_predictors(), threshold = 0.7, method = "spearman")

#Make workflow
rfWorkflow <- workflow() |> 
  add_model(rfMod) |>
  add_recipe(clustRecipe)

#Fit model
clustRfFit <- rfWorkflow |> 
  fit(data = datTrain)

# extract importance from model
clustVarImp <- tibble(predictor = names(extract_fit_parsnip(clustRfFit)$fit$variable.importance),
                  IncMSE = extract_fit_parsnip(clustRfFit)$fit$variable.importance,
                  OOBmse = extract_fit_parsnip(clustRfFit)$fit$prediction.error,
                  OOBr2 = extract_fit_parsnip(clustRfFit)$fit$r.squared) |> 
  # order most to least importance (higher value = greater influence on predictors)
  arrange(-IncMSE) |>  
  # make a factor so they plot in order
  mutate(predictor_factor = factor(predictor, levels = .$predictor))

ggplot(clustVarImp, aes(x = predictor_factor, y = IncMSE)) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_few() +
  labs(title = "DO % Sat Variable Importance")+
  theme(axis.text = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) -> doVarImp
doVarImp
ggsave(filename = "doVarImp.png", plot = doVarImp, path = here("Figs/lmFigs/"), device = "png", width = 21, height = 15, units = "cm", dpi = "retina")

#get predictions from model
datTest$clustPred <- predict(clustRfFit, datTest)$.pred
#Plot
ggplot(datTest, aes(clustPred, avgDO.pctsat)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = T, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "RF Model") +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Predicted DO (% Sat)") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Observed DO (% Sat)") +
  stat_regline_equation(label.y = 2.5, aes(label = ..rr.label..))+
  #coord_equal() +
  theme_few() -> doRf
doRf 
ggsave(filename = "doRf.png", plot = doRf, path = here("Figs/lmFigs/"), device = "png", width = 21, height = 15, units = "cm", dpi = "retina")


```

```{r}
response_vars <- c("Site", "avgDO.pctsat")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "dPercWet_1d", "dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"
)

rfDat <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars)))

# Split data into training and testing sets
set.seed(123)
datSplit <- initial_split(rfDat, prop = 0.75)
datTrain <- training(datSplit)
datTest <- testing(datSplit)

# Define random forest model with tuning
rfMod <- 
  rand_forest(
    mtry = tune(), # Number of predictors to consider at each split
    trees = tune(), # Number of trees
    min_n = tune()  # Minimum number of data points in a node
  ) |> 
  set_engine("ranger", 
             num.threads = (parallel::detectCores() - 1),
             importance = "permutation") |> 
  set_mode("regression")

# Preprocessing
clustRecipe <- datTrain |> 
  recipe(avgDO.pctsat ~ .) |> # Predict avgDO.pctsat based on all predictors
  update_role(Site, new_role = "ID") |> # Set Site as an ID variable
  step_naomit(everything(), skip = TRUE) |> 
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) |> 
  step_corr(all_predictors(), threshold = 0.7, method = "spearman")

# Define workflow
rfWorkflow <- workflow() |> 
  add_model(rfMod) |>
  add_recipe(clustRecipe)

# Define tuning grid
rfGrid <- grid_regular(
  mtry(range = c(2, length(predictor_vars))), # Tune mtry from 2 to total predictors
  trees(range = c(100, 1000)),               # Number of trees from 100 to 1000
  min_n(range = c(5, 20)),                   # Minimum node size from 5 to 20
  levels = 5                                 # Number of levels for each parameter
)

# Perform cross-validation
set.seed(123)
cv_folds <- vfold_cv(datTrain, v = 5) # 5-fold cross-validation

# Tune the model
set.seed(123)
rfTuned <- rfWorkflow |>
  tune_grid(
    resamples = cv_folds,
    grid = rfGrid,
    metrics = metric_set(rmse, rsq) # Metrics to evaluate performance
  )

# Extract the best hyperparameters
best_params <- rfTuned |> 
  select_best(metric = "rmse")

# Finalize the workflow with the best parameters
finalRfWorkflow <- rfWorkflow |> 
  finalize_workflow(best_params)

# Fit the final model
finalRfFit <- finalRfWorkflow |>
  fit(data = datTrain)

# Evaluate the final model on the test set
finalRfResults <- finalRfFit |>
  predict(new_data = datTest) |>
  bind_cols(datTest) |>
  metrics(truth = avgDO.pctsat, estimate = .pred)

# Extract variable importance
final_model <- finalRfFit |> extract_fit_parsnip() |> pluck("fit")
variable_importance <- as.data.frame(final_model$variable.importance) |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "Variable") |>
  rename(Importance = `final_model$variable.importance`) |>
  arrange(desc(Importance))

# Plot variable importance
ggplot(variable_importance, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance",
    x = "Predictor Variables",
    y = "Importance"
  ) +
  theme_few()

# Create predictions for the test set
test_predictions <- finalRfFit |>
  predict(new_data = datTest) |>
  bind_cols(datTest)
# Calculate R-squared
library(broom)
model_metrics <- test_predictions |>
  metrics(truth = avgDO.pctsat, estimate = .pred)

r_squared <- model_metrics |>
  filter(.metric == "rsq") |>
  pull(.estimate)

# Plot observed vs predicted with R-squared
ggplot(test_predictions, aes(x = avgDO.pctsat, y = .pred)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "blue", linetype = "solid", se = T) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -Inf, label = paste0("R² = ", round(r_squared, 3)),
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = -1.5, size = 5, color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Observed vs Predicted",
    x = "Observed avgDO.pctsat",
    y = "Predicted avgDO.pctsat"
  ) +
  theme_few()
```

## 

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(tidymodels)
library(ranger)
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
response_vars <- c("Site", "cluster")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "dPercWet_1d", "dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"
)

rfDat <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  mutate(cluster = factor(cluster))

# Split data into training and testing sets
set.seed(123)
datSplit <- initial_split(rfDat, prop = 0.75)
datTrain <- training(datSplit)
datTest <- testing(datSplit)

# Define random forest model with tuning
rfMod <- 
  rand_forest(
    mtry = tune(), # Number of predictors to consider at each split
    trees = tune(), # Number of trees
    min_n = tune()  # Minimum number of data points in a node
  ) |> 
  set_engine("ranger", 
             num.threads = (parallel::detectCores() - 1),
             importance = "permutation") |> 
  set_mode("classification")

# Preprocessing
clustRecipe <- datTrain |> 
  recipe(cluster ~ .) |> # Predict avgDO.pctsat based on all predictors
  update_role(Site, new_role = "ID") |> # Set Site as an ID variable
  step_naomit(everything(), skip = TRUE) |> 
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) |> 
  step_corr(all_predictors(), threshold = 0.7, method = "spearman")

# Define workflow
rfWorkflow <- workflow() |> 
  add_model(rfMod) |>
  add_recipe(clustRecipe)

# Define tuning grid
rfGrid <- grid_regular(
  mtry(range = c(2, length(predictor_vars))), # Tune mtry from 2 to total predictors
  trees(range = c(100, 1000)),               # Number of trees from 100 to 1000
  min_n(range = c(5, 20)),                   # Minimum node size from 5 to 20
  levels = 5                                 # Number of levels for each parameter
)

# Perform cross-validation
set.seed(123)
cv_folds <- vfold_cv(datTrain, v = 5) # 5-fold cross-validation

# Tune the model
set.seed(123)
rfTuned <- rfWorkflow |>
  tune_grid(
    resamples = cv_folds,
    grid = rfGrid,
  )

# Extract the best hyperparameters
best_params <- rfTuned |> 
  select_best(metric = "rmse")

# Finalize the workflow with the best parameters
finalRfWorkflow <- rfWorkflow |> 
  finalize_workflow(best_params)

# Fit the final model
finalRfFit <- finalRfWorkflow |>
  fit(data = datTrain)

# Evaluate the final model on the test set
finalRfResults <- finalRfFit |>
  predict(new_data = datTest) |>
  bind_cols(datTest) |>
  metrics(truth = cluster, estimate = .pred)

# Extract variable importance
final_model <- finalRfFit |> extract_fit_parsnip() |> pluck("fit")
variable_importance <- as.data.frame(final_model$variable.importance) |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "Variable") |>
  rename(Importance = `final_model$variable.importance`) |>
  arrange(desc(Importance))

# Plot variable importance
ggplot(variable_importance, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance",
    x = "Predictor Variables",
    y = "Importance"
  ) +
  theme_few()

# Create predictions for the test set
test_predictions <- finalRfFit |>
  predict(new_data = datTest) |>
  bind_cols(datTest)
# Calculate R-squared
library(broom)
model_metrics <- test_predictions |>
  metrics(truth = cluster, estimate = .pred)

r_squared <- model_metrics |>
  filter(.metric == "rsq") |>
  pull(.estimate)

# Plot observed vs predicted with R-squared
ggplot(test_predictions, aes(x = cluster, y = .pred)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "blue", linetype = "solid", se = T) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -Inf, label = paste0("R² = ", round(r_squared, 3)),
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = -1.5, size = 5, color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Observed vs Predicted",
    x = "Obs Cluster",
    y = "Pred Cluster"
  ) +
  theme_few()
```

# rda

```{r}
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat", "cluster")

predDat <- dailyDat |> 
  drop_na() |> 
  select(all_of(c(predictor_vars))) |> 
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |> 
  select(where(is.numeric)) |> 
  decostand(method='standardize')

respDat <- dailyDat |> 
  drop_na() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars))) |> 
  mutate(cluster = as.factor(cluster)) |> 
  select(where(is.numeric)) |> 
  decostand(method='standardize') |> 
  as.matrix()
dat.rda <- rda(respDat ~., data = predDat)
summary(dat.rda)
plot(dat.rda)
anova(dat.rda, by='term', permutations=1000)

fwd.sel<-ordiR2step(rda(respDat~1, data=predDat), #lower model limit
  scope=formula(dat.rda), #this is the full model (above)
  direction='forward',
  R2scope=TRUE, #cannot surpass the full models R2
  pstep=1000,
  trace=TRUE)
```

# LM

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat", "PC1", "PC2", "cluster")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)

# Step 2: Correct and define the grouping structure
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d"),
  PPT = c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d"),
  Q = c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d"),
  excProb = c("excProb"),
  #quantQ = c("quantQ", "quantileQ_7d", "quantileQ_14d", "quantileQ_30d"),
  Depth = c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
)

# Step 3: Generate combinations efficiently
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:4, ~combn(predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    valid_groups   # Allow only valid combinations
  })

  return(filtered_combos)
}

# Step 4: Generate the valid predictor combinations
predictor_combos <- generate_filtered_combos()

# Step 5: Create functions to run models
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data) {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared, predictors = predictors, response = response)
}

# Step 6: Prepare data
metDat_model <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 7: Run models for each response and predictor combination
results <- lapply(response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data)
  })
})


# Step 8: Extract results into dataframes
extract_results <- function(results_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(results_list, function(res) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(res, function(model_result) {
      data.frame(
        response = model_result$response,
        predictors = paste(model_result$predictors, collapse = ", "),
        rmse = model_result$rmse,
        r_squared = model_result$r_squared
      )
    }))
  }))
}

results <- extract_results(results) 


# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLM <- results |>
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(response, rmse, r_squared) |> 
  slice_head(n = 5)
```

```{r}

library(furrr)
library(purrr)
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat", "PC1", "PC2", "cluster")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)

# Step 2: Correct and define the grouping structure
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d"),
  PPT = c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d"),
  Q = c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d"),
  excProb = c("excProb"),
  #quantQ = c("quantQ", "quantileQ_7d", "quantileQ_14d", "quantileQ_30d"),
  Depth = c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
)

# Step 3: Generate combinations efficiently
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:4, ~combn(predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    valid_groups   # Allow only valid combinations
  })

  return(filtered_combos)
}

# Step 4: Generate the valid predictor combinations
predictor_combos <- generate_filtered_combos()
# Step 5: Create functions to run models
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, model_type = "linear") {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Apply transformations based on model type
  if (model_type == "log-log") {
    train_data <- train_data |>
      mutate(across(all_of(c(response, predictors)) & -WaterYear, ~ log(. + 1)))
    test_data <- test_data |>
      mutate(across(all_of(c(response, predictors)) & -WaterYear, ~ log(. + 1)))

  } else if (model_type == "log-linear") {
    train_data <- train_data |>
      mutate(across(all_of(c(predictors)) & -WaterYear, ~ log(. + 1)))
    test_data <- test_data |>
      mutate(across(all_of(c(predictors)) & -WaterYear, ~ log(. + 1)))
  } else if (model_type == "linear-log") {
    train_data <- train_data |>
      mutate(across(all_of(response), ~ log(. + 1)))
    test_data <- test_data |>
      mutate(across(all_of(response), ~ log(. + 1)))
  }
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Back-transform predictions if necessary
  if (model_type %in% c("log-log", "log-linear")) {
    predictions <- exp(predictions) - 1
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(
    model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared,
    predictors = predictors, response = response, model_type = model_type
  )
}

# Step 6: Prepare data
metDat_model <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 7: Run models for each response and predictor combination with different types (parallelized)
plan(multisession, workers = 6)  # Set up parallel processing

# Define a function for a single model run to be used with furrr
run_all_models <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, model_types) {
  lapply(model_types, function(model_type) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, model_type)
  })
}

# Define model_types globally before the parallel map
model_types <- c("linear", "log-log", "log-linear", "linear-log")

# Parallelized execution with furrr
results <- future_map(
  response_vars, 
  function(response) {
    future_map(
      predictor_combos, 
      function(predictors) {
        run_all_models(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, model_types)
      }
    )
  }
)


# Step 8: Extract results into dataframes (unchanged)
extract_results <- function(results_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(results_list, function(res) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(res, function(model_res) {
      do.call(rbind, lapply(model_res, function(model_result) {
        data.frame(
          response = model_result$response,
          predictors = paste(model_result$predictors, collapse = ", "),
          model_type = model_result$model_type,
          rmse = model_result$rmse,
          r_squared = model_result$r_squared
        )
      }))
    }))
  }))
}

results <- extract_results(results)

# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLM <- results |>
  group_by(response, model_type) |>
  arrange(response, model_type, rmse, r_squared) |>
  slice_head(n = 5)

# Shut down the parallel backend when done
plan(sequential)
```

## Log-linear

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat", "PC1", "PC2", "cluster")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)

# Step 2: Correct and define the grouping structure
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d"),
  PPT = c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d"),
  Q = c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d"),
  excProb = c("excProb"),
  #quantQ = c("quantQ", "quantileQ_7d", "quantileQ_14d", "quantileQ_30d"),
  Depth = c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
)

# Step 3: Generate combinations efficiently
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:4, ~combn(predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    valid_groups   # Allow only valid combinations
  })

  return(filtered_combos)
}

# Step 4: Generate the valid predictor combinations
predictor_combos <- generate_filtered_combos()

# Step 5: Create functions to run models
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data) {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared, predictors = predictors, response = response)
}

# Step 6: Prepare data with log-transformed responses
metDat_model <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  # Apply log transformation to the response variables
  mutate(across(all_of(response_vars), ~ log(. + 0.01), .names = "log_{col}")) |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |> 
  mutate(across(all_of(predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"]), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Update response variables to use log-transformed versions
log_response_vars <- paste0("log_", response_vars)

# Step 7: Run models for each log-transformed response and predictor combination
results <- lapply(log_response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data)
  })
})


results <- extract_results(results)


# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLogLin <- results |>
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(response, rmse, r_squared) |> 
  slice_head(n = 5)
```

## Linear-log

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat", "PC1", "PC2", "cluster")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)


# Update predictor variables to use log-transformed versions
log_predictor_vars <- paste0("log_", predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"])

# Update grouped predictors to use log-transformed variable names
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = paste0("log_", c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d")),
  PPT = paste0("log_", c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")),
  Q = paste0("log_", c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d")),
  excProb = paste0("log_", c("excProb")),
  Depth = paste0("log_", c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"))
)


# Step 3 (modified): Generate combinations efficiently with log-transformed predictors
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:5, ~combn(log_predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    
    valid_groups  # Allow only valid combinations
  })
  
  return(filtered_combos)
}


# Step 5: Create functions to run models with and without interaction terms
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data) {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
```
